perturbation.original_text: (c) "Affiliate(s)" shall mean an entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a party. "Control," for purposes of this definition, means direct or indirect ownership or control of more than 50% of the voting interests of the subject entity.
perturbation.changed_text: (c) "Affiliate(s)" shall mean an entity controlling, controlled by or under common control with a party. "Control," for purposes of this definition, means direct or indirect ownership or control of more than 25% of the voting interests of the subject entity.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by altering the definition of 'Control' within the definition of 'Affiliate(s)'. Initially, 'Control' meant ownership of over 50% of voting interests, now it means ownership of over 25% of voting interests. This in-text contradiction means that subsequent sections relying on the definition of 'Affiliate(s)' will be unclear on the ownership threshold required to establish an affiliate relationship, making the application of the contract ambiguous and potentially leading to disputes, for example Section 13 Indemnification or Section 17(h) Assignment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party shall defend, indemnify and hold the other party and its Affiliates, and their respective directors, officers, employees, agents, successors, assigns, licensees and distributors harmless from and against any and all judgments, settlements, damages, penalties, costs and expenses (including, but not limited to, reasonable attorneys' fees) arising out of any third party claims relating to the Indemnifying Party's breach or alleged breach of any of its representations, warranties, covenants or obligations hereunder. The party seeking indemnification (the "Indemnified Party") will give prompt notice to the indemnifying party (the "Indemnifying Party") of any claim for which the Indemnified Party seeks indemnification under this Agreement; provided, however, that failure to give prompt notice will not relieve the Indemnifying Party of any liability hereunder (except to the extent the Indemnifying Party has suffered actual material prejudice by such failure). The Indemnified Party will reasonably cooperate (at the Indemnifying Party's expense) in the defense of any claim for which the Indemnified Party seeks indemnification under this Section 13. The Indemnified Party shall have the right to employ separate counsel and to participate in (but not control) any such action, but the fees and expenses of such counsel will be at the expense of the Indemnified Party unless: (i) the employment of counsel by the Indemnified Party has been authorized by the Indemnifying Party; (ii) the Indemnified Party has been advised by its counsel in writing that there is a conflict of interest between the Indemnifying Party and the Indemnified Party in the conduct of the defense of the action (in which case the Indemnifying Party will not have the right to direct the defense of the action on behalf of the Indemnified Party); or (iii) the Indemnifying Party has not in fact employed counsel to assume the defense of the action within a reasonable time following receipt of the notice given pursuant to this Section 13, in each of which cases the fees and expenses of such counsel will be at the expense of the Indemnifying Party. The Indemnifying Party will not be liable for any settlement of an action effected without its written consent (which consent will not be unreasonably withheld or delayed), nor will the Indemnifying Party settle any such action without the written consent of the Indemnified Party (which consent will not be unreasonably withheld or delayed). The Indemnifying Party will not consent to the entry of any judgment or enter into any settlement that does not include as an unconditional term thereof the giving by the claimant or plaintiff to the Indemnified Party a release from all liability with respect to the claim.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of 'Control' from ownership of over 50% to over 25% of voting interests. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which relies on the original definition of 'Affiliates' and 'Control' for indemnification obligations. The change introduces ambiguity and directly conflicts with Section 13 (Indemnification) and potentially Section 17(h) (Assignment), as the scope of 'Affiliates' has now been broadened without explicitly updating these dependent sections. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as to who is covered under the indemnification clause, making enforcement and interpretation difficult.

perturbation.original_text: The Term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date listed above and continue for twenty (20) years, unless sooner terminated as provided in Section 7(b).
perturbation.changed_text: The Term of this Agreement (the "Term") shall commence on the Effective Date listed above and continue for five (5) years, unless sooner terminated as provided in Section 7(b).
perturbation.explanation: The modification of the term length from twenty (20) years to five (5) years creates an in-text contradiction, specifically impacting Sections 2(a) and 7(c). Section 2(a) License Grant, states that the Licensor grants to Licensee a non-exclusive, royalty-free, perpetual and non-perpetual license, subject to the duration for which Licensor has the rights to each Title as specified in Schedule A1-A5 of Schedule A. Furthermore Section 7(c) specifies the survival of certain sections post-termination, depending on whether the licenses granted in Section 2(a) are perpetual or for a specific duration. This in-text contradiction regarding the overall term length creates significant uncertainty regarding the licenses being perpetual, meaning they will survive beyond the five (5) years of the agreement's term, leading to potential disputes and impairing the enforceability of rights and obligations after the modified five-year term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sections 2(a), 2(b), 2(c), 2(d), 3, and 11 shall survive the expiration or termination of this Agreement: (i) in perpetuity with respect to Titles for which the licenses granted in Section 2(a) are perpetual; and (ii) for the duration of the applicable license term specified in Schedule A with respect to Titles for which the license term specified in Schedule A extends beyond the expiration or termination of this Agreement. Sections 1, 7, 8(a), 12, 13, 14, 15, 16 and 17 shall survive any expiration or termination of this Agreement in perpetuity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement term from twenty (20) years to five (5) years. This directly contradicts the contradicted text. The contradicted text specifies that certain sections survive based on whether the licenses granted are perpetual or for a specific duration. Since the original term was twenty years, the perpetual licenses would only apply to licenses granted for more than twenty years. By changing the term to five years, it affects the duration of non-perpetual licenses and survival of terms impacting the licenses, thus creating an in-text contradiction. Therefore, the modification directly clashes with the contradicted text, impacting the enforceability of rights and obligations after the five-year term.

perturbation.original_text: No royalty or fees of any kind shall be owed by Licensee under this Agreement. The consideration for the licenses granted by Licensor to Licensee under this Agreement is the issuance of the IP Common Shares as defined in the Securities Purchase Agreement, dated as of November 23, 2015, by and among the Licensee and the Licensor (the "Share Consideration").
perturbation.changed_text: No royalty or fees of any kind shall be owed by Licensee under this Agreement, except for a one-time fee of $10,000 USD for each title under schedule A. The consideration for the licenses granted by Licensor to Licensee under this Agreement is the issuance of the IP Common Shares as defined in the Securities Purchase Agreement, dated as of November 23, 2015, by and among the Licensee and the Licensor (the "Share Consideration").
perturbation.explanation: The added clause "except for a one-time fee of $10,000 USD for each title under schedule A" creates an in-text contradiction. The first sentence explicitly states that no royalty or fees of any kind shall be owed. However, the added clause imposes a fee, causing uncertainty about the actual payment obligations. Section 12(a)(v) states 'There are no taxes, charges, fees, royalties or other amounts owed to any party other than as set forth in this Agreement for the exercise of rights granted in this Agreement'. With this perturbation now there exists an amount, not previously set forth in the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Licensor represents and warrants that: ...v. There are no taxes, charges, fees, royalties or other amounts owed to any party other than as set forth in this Agreement for the exercise of rights granted in this Agreement and Licensor has paid or will pay all charges, taxes, license fees and other amounts that have been or may become owed in connection with the Titles or the exercise of any rights granted under this Agreement;
perturbation.justification: The original text states that no royalties or fees are owed, while the changed text introduces a one-time fee. The contradicted text warrants that no amounts are owed other than as set forth in the agreement. The modified text directly contradicts this warranty by introducing a fee that was not previously set forth, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction regarding payment obligations. The added clause "except for a one-time fee of $10,000 USD for each title under schedule A" creates a direct conflict.
